result:
**Goal: Add a real-time rule mutation engine that continuously evolves simulation rules toward maximum visual complexity using entropy feedback.**

The simulator already has entropy/diversity analytics, a rule editor, and genome infrastructure — but no mode that *autonomously breeds rules in real-time*, mutating birth/survival sets (and mode-specific parameters) each generation, keeping mutations that increase Shannon entropy and spatial complexity, and reverting ones that lead to extinction or static states. Think of it as a "genetic algorithm for interesting-ness" running live on screen — the user watches as the simulation discovers its own most visually complex behaviors, with a lineage sidebar showing the ancestry of the current rule. This turns the simulator from a tool you explore manually into one that explores *itself*.

Shall I build it?
